summary: THE cu130 ship (from MPI-187). Rebuild product Pod image on ONE cu130 base, collapse the cu124/cu128 two-profile split. Coverage fear FALSE (live-checked: 4090+5090 both cu130-available). Multi-arch sage TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=8.6;8.9;12.0, pin to cu130-good commit, non-blocking (SDPA fallback). Kill the stale MPI-156 do-not-cu130 reasoning in the Dockerfile. 2nd of sequential 3 (MPI-188 guard first, then this, then MPI-190 bf16).

Now GATED ON MPI-186: 186 decides the image architecture (bake-and-cache + shrink), 189 builds it on cu130. Order: 186 -> 189 -> 188 (guard, alongside) -> 190 (bf16). Shrink+bake land in this same rebuild.

UNGATED — MPI-186 concluded. Card body now carries the full MPI-186 decision: build ONE cu130 image on a -runtime base via multi-stage Dockerfile (sage compiled in a -devel stage), push to Docker Hub (not GHCR), repoint POD_IMAGE_BASE + CI. Shrink (~13GB uncompressed: nightly-torch + CUDA -dev toolkit) + registry move land in THIS rebuild. Startable from tasks/MPI-186/research/decision.md. Prereq MPI-188 already done.

Compact plan attached (plan.md). Locked decisions: base = runpod/pytorch cu130 -runtime, MULTI-STAGE (sage built in a -devel stage, ship -runtime); sage multi-arch 8.6;8.9;12.0 non-blocking (ship cu130 + SDPA fallback if it wont build); keep ComfyUI 0.27 (not the RunPod image's 0.26). CORRECTED torch: proven stack = torch 2.10.0+cu130 (NOT 2.11/2.12) — the lever is the +cu130 BUILD not the torch minor (2.10+cu130=~11s, 2.11+cu126=126s). 6 sub-parts one flow: Dockerfile single-cu130 multi-stage + delete stale cu126 comments; sage; CI matrix collapse + GHCR->Docker Hub push; app podImageForCard/POD_IMAGE_BASE/allowedCudaVersions collapse; build+ship (USER live, watch CI overflow -> local Flow B); docs. Verify mode user-ux (live 4090+5090 deploy, ~11s fault-in, MPI-188 guard). Stays in todo/planned — implementation runs in a FRESH session via mpi-continue.

todo->doing. Executing code edits (all 6 sub-parts except live build/deploy which are USER-only). Scope confirmed: multi-stage cu130 Dockerfile + CI matrix collapse + GHCR->Docker Hub + app podImageForCard/POD_IMAGE_VERSION/podCudaFloor collapse + docs. Docker Hub = test-vs-GHCR (agent sets up repo/secrets, user does interactive signup).

ALL CODE EDITS DONE (5 files, verified on disk). Dockerfile=single cu130 on nvidia/cuda:13.0.3-runtime (torch 2.10.0+cu130, uninstall-first + assert +cu130 guard; deleted cu124/cu128 branches + stale MPI-156 cu126 block + sage source-build); buildx --check clean + base metadata resolved. App=podImageForCard one -cu130 tag, POD_IMAGE_VERSION v0.12.0, POD_IMAGE_BASE->Docker Hub + POD_IMAGE_BASE_CPU (GHCR), podCudaFloor flat [13.0]; node --check OK. CI=matrix cu124+cu128->cu130, per-row registry, two gated logins (DockerHub cu130 / GHCR cpu). Docs=02-image-and-rebuild.md + README (incl user-requested sage-deferred rationale). DRIFT: base flipped runpod/pytorch->nvidia/cuda (no cu130 runpod tag exists); sage DROPPED this build (user-approved, SDPA fallback, #157 makes source build unreliable) -> NO multi-stage needed, single-stage. REMAINING = USER live ops only: Docker Hub repo+secrets (interactive), commit+push, build+push, live-verify 4090+5090 ~11s. Stays doing.

BUILD: pushed both repos. CI dispatched (run 28715218897): cpu leg SUCCESS->GHCR (Docker Hub secrets confirmed working via the login step); cu130 leg FAILED at ComfyUI clone (git clone --branch rejects the commit SHA I passed, exit 128 — NOT disk overflow). Root cause = wrong input (SHA vs TAG), fixed skill: <ref> must be lock TAG v0.27.0. Per user: cu130 now builds LOCAL, cpu stays CI. Local cu130 build running (bg), v0.12.0-cu130, COMFYUI_REF=v0.27.0. Push pending Docker Hub login (agent shell no-TTY; user GUI-login or one docker login).

BUILD SHIPPED via CI (run 28716193726, TAG v0.27.0 fixed the clone). Both tags SUCCESS + public-pullable: docker.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.12.0-cu130 (Docker Hub) + ghcr .../:v0.12.0-cpu (GHCR). SHRINK CONFIRMED: cu130 compressed pull = 11.13GB / 31 layers vs old cu128 ~18-20GB — ~40%, beats MPI-186 30% target (nvidia/cuda -runtime base bakes no torch/toolkit + no sage). Local build (raced) cancelled after CI won. Remaining: torch +cu130 assert (pull running) + USER live-deploy 4090+5090 for ~11s fault-in.

LIVE-PROVEN BOTH ARCHES 2026-07-04. Single cu130 image boots + gens on 4090 (Ada sm_89) AND 5090 (Blackwell sm_120) — one-image thesis CONFIRMED. Boot markers perfect both: aimdo inited + DynamicVRAM enabled + torch 2.10.0+cu130 + ComfyUI 0.27 + SDPA (no sage, non-fatal). MPI-188 r580 floor landed correct hosts (driver 580.x/CUDA13). NO HANG between stages (the core worry) — clean on both. Cold-start pull 1-2.5min (was ~5min); compressed 11GB (~40% shrink, beat 30% target). 5090 WARM gen = 24.9s clean, video out. FINDING (feeds MPI-190): GGUF cold transformer load ~130s (dequant cost, NOT cu130) + OOMs in dequant on 24GB 4090 (MPI-185), fits 32GB 5090. bf16 revert (MPI-190) removes dequant entirely -> no cold tax + no OOM + smooth offload + better quality. Hard evidence for MPI-190 now in hand.

doing->done (complete/accepted). Single cu130 image built + pushed (Docker Hub) + LIVE-PROVEN both arches (4090 Ada + 5090 Blackwell), no hang between stages, shrink ~40% + registry move shipped. ~11s fault-in criterion ROLLS to MPI-190: it was always a COLD-bf16 number (RunPod-official test); that test never got a warm baseline (first run failed). Our GGUF cold=~130s (dequant, not cu130), warm=24.9s (fresh GGUF baseline, not comparable to ~11s). MPI-190 (bf16 revert) gives the apples-to-apples cold-bf16-on-our-cu130 test. 190 backed by hard evidence: GGUF dequant OOMs 24GB + ~130s cold; bf16 removes dequant.
